creator: salvadore@freebsd.org
text:
The issue reported is a known issue which is explained in pkg-help: building the port with LTO_BOOTSTRAP enabled requires a significant amount of ram. Please see also bug #264949.

The port is confirmed to build successfully on the packages builders, which have LTO_BOOTSTRAP enabled. Please see https://pkg-status.freebsd.org/beefy16/data/131amd64-default/634ed29b7bad/logs/gcc12-12.2.0_5.log .

If you want to try again building GCC 12 on your machine, I suggest trying to save as much ram as possible. I see you are using poudriere: you might want to ensure that no other ports are build while you compile GCC 12 by limiting the number of parallel jobs to 1.
